title: polypodium undulatum var. parvulum
intro: Polypodium undulatum var. parvulum, also known as New Zealand fern, is a small, creeping fern native to the Pacific Islands and New Zealand. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Polypodium undulatum var. parvulum include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and support cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in topical creams and salves for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
